title: Running a Reflect check-in
what_it_is: Reflect is a built-in Teams tool that lets you ask your class a quick question and get an answer back from each learner privately, without anyone else in the group seeing how they responded.
why_use_it: A Reflect check-in gives every learner a private way to answer, so you hear from people who might not put their hand up or speak out in front of the group.
how_to: Open your class team in Teams.
Select the Reflect tab at the top of the team. If you can't see it, select the + to the right of your existing tabs and search for Reflect to add it.
Type your question, then choose the response options learners will pick from. Reflect includes ready-made options, such as an emoji-based mood scale, or you can write your own.
Set a date and time for the check-in to open, then select Send.
Learners open the check-in from their own Teams app, select the response that fits, and submit it. Their individual answer is only visible to you, not to the rest of the class.
To see the results, open the check-in from the Reflect tab. Responses are shown as a chart of how many learners picked each option, without naming who picked what.
